focus: Q4: Wire the mode switch into loop.cjs — implement the actual code changes including reduce-state.cjs, converge.cjs, and ensure materialize-benchmark-fixtures.cjs is only invoked for model-benchmark mode.

label: deep-agent-improvement has no loop.cjs - the loop is journal-serialized orchestration via improvement-journal.cjs, reduce-state.cjs, promote-candidate.cjs
materialize-benchmark-fixtures.cjs is standalone and gated - not called by run-benchmark.cjs; must be invoked separately before run-benchmark in model-benchmark mode
reduce-state.cjs is mode-agnostic - only consumes state records by type; no structural changes needed for mode switch
converge.cjs is absent - convergence is in promote-candidate.cjs which uses score + stability + benchmark gates for agent-improvement and aggregateScore/passRate for model-benchmark
Mode switch entry point must be a new loop-host.cjs that gates dispatch: agent-improvement path calls score-candidate.cjs; model-benchmark path calls materialize then run-benchmark then promote
reduce-state.cjs minimal change: add mode field pass-through and display in dashboard; no structural rewrite needed
promote-candidate.cjs needs mode-aware convergence: agent-improvement uses WEIGHTED_SCORE_GATE+stability+benchmark-pass; model-benchmark uses aggregateScore+minimumFixtureScore+passRate
Backward-compat invariant: loop-host.cjs with mode=agent-improvement (or absent) must produce byte-identical state JSONL as current score-candidate.cjs path

reason: reduce-state is purely reactive; mode detection belongs upstream in the loop host that writes state records

direction: modifying materialize-benchmark-fixtures.cjs to check mode internally
reason: materialize should remain a pure materializer; gating belongs to the caller (loop-host.cjs)
